page: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is a virtual machine instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 96 vCPUs based on the AMD x86_64 architecture, configured with a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance does not include any dedicated graphics processing units. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, indicating that primary storage requirements must be met via external network storage volumes. The configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Because the 96 vCPUs are allocated on a shared basis, this instance offers high resource density and a lower cost profile relative to dedicated hardware of similar scale. However, the shared CPU model introduces potential performance variability due to resource contention from other tenants on the host. This server is designed for large-scale general-purpose workloads, batch processing, and memory-intensive applications that can tolerate CPU scheduling variations rather than sustained, latency-sensitive compute tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is a virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 96 shared AMD vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The instance includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage, zero GPUs, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Because the CPU resources are shared, this instance delivers high memory and core density at a lower cost tier, though it introduces potential performance variability. It is designed for large-scale general-purpose workloads, offline batch processing, and memory-intensive tasks that do not require dedicated, sustained CPU performance.

tagline: A high-density virtual server offering ninety-six shared AMD cores and balanced memory for large-scale, budget-conscious general-purpose workloads.
